notes: Precedence controls grouping, not evaluation order.
Evaluation order of operands is always left-to-right.
Short-circuiting operators may skip evaluation of later operands.

evaluationOrder.assignment: evaluate reference base/index first, then RHS, then perform assignment
evaluationOrder.conditional: test, then only consequent or alternate

operators.name: grouping
notes: Controls grouping only; does not change left-to-right evaluation of subexpressions.

operators.name: memberAccess
notes: Base expression evaluated before property/index.
For obj[expr], obj is evaluated before expr.

operators.name: callAndOptionalChaining
notes: Function arguments evaluated left-to-right.
Optional chaining short-circuits when base is null or undefined, skipping property/index/arguments.

operators.name: newWithoutArgs
notes: new X has higher precedence than new X().

operators.name: postfixIncrementDecrement
notes: Operand evaluated first; result is old value; side effect applied after value is produced.

operators.name: unary
notes: Operand evaluated after operator is chosen; right-associative for chains like !+x.

operators.name: exponentiation
notes: Right-associative: a ** b ** c == a ** (b ** c).
Left operand evaluated before right operand.

operators.name: additive
notes: + is also string concatenation depending on operand types.

operators.name: logicalAnd
notes: Evaluate left operand; if falsy, return it and skip right operand.
Returns one of the operands, not necessarily boolean.

operators.name: logicalOr
notes: Evaluate left operand; if truthy, return it and skip right operand.
Returns one of the operands, not necessarily boolean.

operators.name: nullishCoalescing
notes: Evaluate left operand; if not null/undefined, return it and skip right operand.
Otherwise evaluate and return right operand.

operators.name: conditional
notes: Evaluate test; then only consequent or alternate is evaluated.
Right-associative: a ? b : c ? d : e == a ? b : (c ? d : e).

operators.name: assignment
notes: Evaluate LHS reference base/index first, then RHS.
Logical assignments (&&=, ||=, ??=) short-circuit based on current LHS value.

operators.name: comma
notes: Evaluate left expression, then right; result is right expression's value.
